_meta.dna: linux(native) = appimage(portable) x wayland(modern) x x11(compatible) x distros(ubuntu+fedora+arch) x flatpak(sandboxed) x cli(first_class) x systemd(managed)
_meta.question_expansion_instruction: Each floor lists 7 seed questions. Linux users are power users, developers, and enterprise server admins. They expect CLI-first workflows, proper packaging, and no Windows/Mac assumptions. Every question must probe real Linux compatibility.

name: Tower of Linux
protocol: FORECAST -> CROSS-REF -> AUDIT -> FIX -> VERIFY -> SEAL -> POSTMORTEM

questions: Is there an AppImage that runs on any Linux distribution without installation?
Does the AppImage include all dependencies (no system library requirements)?
Is the AppImage signed for verification?
Does the AppImage integrate with the desktop (icons, file associations)?
Can the AppImage auto-update via AppImageUpdate?
Is the AppImage size reasonable (not bloated)?
Does the AppImage handle FUSE mounting correctly?
Is there a .deb package for Debian/Ubuntu users?
Does the .deb install dependencies correctly via apt?
Is there an APT repository for automatic updates?
Does the package follow Debian packaging guidelines?
Is the .deb tested on Ubuntu LTS (22.04, 24.04)?
Does the package create correct desktop entries and icons?
Does the uninstall (apt remove) clean up properly?
Is there a Flatpak available on Flathub?
Does the Flatpak sandbox allow necessary file system access?
Are Flatpak permissions appropriate (not over-permissive)?
Does the Flatpak integrate with the desktop portal system?
Can the Flatpak auto-update through the software center?
Does the Flatpak use shared runtime to minimize size?
Can the Flatpak access the OAuth3 vault through the Secret portal?
Does the app run correctly on Wayland (GNOME, KDE Plasma)?
Is the app using native Wayland or XWayland?
Does screen capture work on Wayland (xdg-desktop-portal)?
Does copy/paste work correctly on Wayland?
Does drag-and-drop work on Wayland?
Are window decorations correct on Wayland (CSD vs SSD)?
Does HiDPI scaling work correctly on Wayland?
Does the app run correctly on X11?
Do keyboard shortcuts work correctly on X11?
Does multi-monitor work on X11 with different DPI?
Does the clipboard work correctly on X11 (CLIPBOARD vs PRIMARY)?
Does the app handle X11 window managers (i3, sway, bspwm)?
Are X11-specific accessibility features supported?
Does the app handle X11 display forwarding (SSH -X)?
Is there an .rpm package for Fedora/RHEL users?
Does the RPM follow Fedora packaging guidelines?
Is there a COPR or YUM/DNF repository?
Does the app work on RHEL 8+ and Fedora latest?
Are SELinux policies compatible with the app?
Does the RPM handle systemd service files?
Is the RPM tested on CentOS Stream?
Is there an AUR package (PKGBUILD) for Arch users?
Does the PKGBUILD build from source or use prebuilt binaries?
Is the package maintained and up to date in AUR?
Does the app work on Arch Linux with latest packages?
Are all dependencies available in the Arch repositories?
Does the app work on Arch derivatives (Manjaro, EndeavourOS)?
Is there documentation for Arch-specific setup?
Is there a systemd user service file for the daemon mode?
Can the service be managed via systemctl --user?
Does the service start on login (systemctl --user enable)?
Does the service handle restart-on-failure correctly?
Are service logs accessible via journalctl?
Does the service use appropriate resource limits (MemoryLimit, CPUQuota)?
Is the service socket-activated for efficiency?
Does the app integrate with GNOME's notification system?
Does the app icon appear correctly in GNOME Activities?
Are GNOME keyboard shortcuts compatible?
Does the app work with GNOME's dark mode preference?
Does the app integrate with GNOME Online Accounts?
Does the app support GNOME's screen reader (Orca)?
Does the app follow GNOME Human Interface Guidelines?
Does the app integrate with KDE's notification system?
Does the app pick up KDE color schemes and themes?
Are KDE keyboard shortcuts compatible?
Does the app work with KDE's system tray?
Does the app integrate with KDE Wallet for credential storage?
Does the app support KDE's file dialog (vs GTK)?
Does the app work on KDE Plasma 6?
Can the entire Solace workflow be driven from the terminal?
Does the CLI work correctly in bash, zsh, fish, and dash?
Are man pages installed for all CLI commands?
Does tab completion work in the user's shell?
Can the CLI output be piped to standard Unix tools (grep, awk, jq)?
Does the CLI respect standard signals (SIGTERM, SIGINT, SIGHUP)?
Can the browser run headless for CI/automation on Linux?
Does the app use XDG directories (XDG_CONFIG_HOME, XDG_DATA_HOME, XDG_CACHE_HOME)?
Is the .desktop file correct and complete?
Does the app register MIME types correctly?
Is the app icon installed in the correct XDG icon locations?
Does the app use xdg-open for opening URLs and files?
Does the app integrate with xdg-desktop-portal for sandboxed operations?
Does the app respect XDG_RUNTIME_DIR for temporary data?
Can Orca screen reader navigate all UI elements?
Are ARIA labels correctly announced by Orca?
Does the YinYang approval dialog work with Orca?
Can Orca users access settings and change themes?
Are focus indicators visible with AT-SPI2?
Does the app expose UI elements through AT-SPI?
Are dynamic content changes announced to Orca?
Does the app render correctly at 2x scaling on Linux?
Does fractional scaling (1.25x, 1.5x) work correctly?
Are icons sharp on HiDPI displays?
Does the app handle mixed-DPI multi-monitor setups?
Does the app respond to DPI changes without restart?
Does the app use correct DPI detection on both X11 and Wayland?
Are there blurry elements at non-integer scaling factors?
Does the app respect fontconfig settings?
Are fonts rendered correctly with subpixel rendering enabled/disabled?
Does the app handle font substitution (fontconfig aliases)?
Are CJK fonts available and rendered correctly?
Does the app handle emoji rendering (color emoji fonts)?
Are monospace fonts rendered correctly in code/terminal areas?
Does the app respect system font size settings?
Does the app handle NetworkManager connection changes?
Does the app work through corporate proxies on Linux?
Can the cloudflared tunnel work on Linux?
Does the app detect captive portals?
Does the app handle VPN connections (OpenVPN, WireGuard)?
Is the app compatible with nftables/iptables firewall rules?
Does the app handle DNS resolution (systemd-resolved, dnsmasq)?
Does the OAuth3 vault integrate with the Secret Service API (GNOME Keyring, KDE Wallet)?
Can the vault use libsecret for cross-desktop credential storage?
Does the vault fallback to encrypted file storage if no secret service?
Are secrets locked when the screen locks?
Can users manage Solace credentials in Seahorse (GNOME) or KWalletManager?
Does the vault handle PAM-based unlocking?
Are credentials cleaned up on app uninstall?
Can Solace Browser run in a Docker container?
Is there an official Docker image for headless/CI use?
Does the container handle X11/Wayland display forwarding?
Can the container access host resources (GPU, network)?
Is the container image size minimal (Alpine-based)?
Can the container run as non-root?
Does Docker Compose support multi-service setup (browser + daemon)?
Does the app work with SELinux in enforcing mode?
Are there AppArmor profiles for the app?
Does the app handle MAC denials gracefully (not crash)?
Are the minimum required permissions documented?
Can IT admins create custom MAC policies for the app?
Does the Flatpak version handle MAC correctly?
Are there audit logs for MAC policy violations?
Is there a Snap package available in the Snap Store?
Does the Snap interface plug system provide necessary access?
Is the Snap auto-updated through the Snap daemon?
Does the Snap sandbox allow OAuth3 vault operations?
Can the Snap access the user's home directory?
Does the Snap work on all distributions that support Snapd?
Is the Snap build reproducible and verifiable?
Does the app work correctly in tiling window managers?
Are floating dialogs (YinYang approval) handled correctly?
Does the app set appropriate window hints (WM_CLASS, window role)?
Can users resize the app window without UI breakage?
Does the app handle being tiled to very small sizes?
Are popups and dropdowns positioned correctly in tiled layouts?
Does the app support urgent/attention hints for notifications?
Is there a Nix package for Solace Browser?
Does the Nix derivation build correctly?
Can the app be configured declaratively in NixOS configuration.nix?
Does the app work with NixOS's immutable filesystem?
Are all dependencies available in nixpkgs?
Does the app handle Nix's non-standard filesystem layout?
Can the app be installed via nix profile or nix-env?
Does the app run on Raspberry Pi 4/5 (ARM64)?
Is the performance acceptable on a Raspberry Pi?
Does the app work with Raspberry Pi OS (Debian-based)?
Can the app run in kiosk mode on a Raspberry Pi?
Does the app handle the Pi's limited RAM (4GB/8GB)?
Is the ARM64 build tested as part of CI?
Can the Raspberry Pi run as a Solace Browser server (headless)?
Does the app use the system print dialog (CUPS)?
Can users print evidence reports from the browser?
Does print preview work correctly on Linux?
Are print margins and page layout appropriate?
Does the app support PDF export via print?
Can users print to network printers via CUPS?
Does the app handle printer discovery correctly?
Can the app play notification sounds through PulseAudio/PipeWire?
Does the app respect system volume settings?
Are sounds played through the correct audio sink?
Does the app handle audio server restarts (PipeWire restart)?
Can users disable app notification sounds?
Does the app handle Bluetooth audio output?
Are sound files embedded or loaded from disk?
Does the daemon scheduler use systemd timers or cron?
Can scheduled tasks run when the user is not logged in?
Are timer logs accessible via journalctl?
Does the scheduler handle system sleep/resume correctly?
Can multiple schedules run concurrently without conflicts?
Are failed scheduled tasks retried with appropriate backoff?
Does the scheduler use efficient wake-up patterns (not polling)?
Does the app use GPU acceleration for rendering?
Is VA-API or VDPAU video decoding supported?
Does the app handle switching between Intel/AMD/NVIDIA GPUs?
Are Vulkan or OpenGL backends used efficiently?
Does the app work on systems without GPU (software rendering)?
Does the NVIDIA proprietary driver work correctly?
Is GPU memory usage bounded?
Does the app register file associations correctly?
Can files be opened with Solace Browser from the file manager?
Does the app appear in 'Open With' menus?
Are MIME type associations correct?
Does drag-and-drop from file managers work?
Does the app create thumbnails for associated file types?
Can the app open URLs from the file manager?
Does the app use DBus for inter-process communication?
Can other applications control Solace Browser via DBus?
Does the app expose status information on DBus?
Are notifications sent via org.freedesktop.Notifications?
Does the app register a DBus service name?
Can the daemon be controlled via DBus commands?
Does the app handle DBus session vs system bus correctly?
Does the app detect battery status and reduce activity?
Does the app handle suspend/resume correctly?
Are idle inhibitors used when performing critical operations?
Does the app work with TLP power management?
Can the daemon scheduler adjust frequency based on power state?
Does the app handle lid close/open events?
Is the app's power consumption reasonable on laptops?
Does the app respect LANG and LC_* environment variables?
Are IBus and Fcitx input methods supported?
Does the app handle system locale changes?
Are date/time/number formats locale-aware?
Does the app support Compose key sequences?
Are dead keys (accented characters) handled correctly?
Does the app support RTL locales on Linux?
Are app data files in standard XDG locations for backup tools?
Can users back up app data with standard tools (tar, rsync)?
Does the app handle restoring from backup gracefully?
Are the vault and evidence chain included in backups?
Can users export/import settings for manual backup?
Does the app handle Timeshift (btrfs snapshot) restores?
Are cache files excluded from typical backup patterns?
Can the browser run headless over SSH?
Does X11 forwarding work for remote GUI access?
Can the CLI operate fully over SSH without display?
Does the app work via VNC or XRDP?
Can the daemon run on a remote server and be controlled locally?
Does the app handle high-latency SSH connections?
Can screen/tmux sessions maintain browser state?
Can users build Solace Browser from source on Linux?
Are all build dependencies available in standard repositories?
Does the build system use standard tools (make, cmake, meson)?
Are build instructions clear and tested?
Does the build produce reproducible output?
Can the build be cross-compiled for different architectures?
Is the build time reasonable on typical developer hardware?
Does the app work on immutable distributions (Silverblue, MicroOS)?
Can the Flatpak version work on immutable distros?
Does the app handle /usr being read-only?
Can the app store data in the user's writable home directory?
Does the app work with Toolbx/Distrobox containers?
Are system-level dependencies avoided on immutable distros?
Does the auto-update mechanism work on immutable distros?
Does the app work in Crostini (Linux on ChromeOS)?
Can the app handle Crostini's limited GPU acceleration?
Does the app work with Crostini's Wayland implementation?
Are file sharing between ChromeOS and Crostini handled?
Does the app handle Crostini's network configuration?
Can the app run in the limited Crostini container?
Is there documentation for ChromeOS setup?
Does the app use namespaces for process isolation?
Are capabilities dropped to minimum required (no root)?
Does the app work with seccomp-bpf filtering?
Is the app compatible with Linux Security Modules (LSM)?
Does the app handle /proc and /sys access securely?
Are SUID/SGID bits avoided in the installation?
Does the app use hardened compilation flags (RELRO, PIE, stack canary)?
Does the app handle low-memory situations gracefully?
Is the app OOM-killer safe (appropriate oom_score_adj)?
Does the app work well with zram/zswap?
Are memory-mapped files used efficiently?
Does the app handle memory pressure notifications (cgroups v2)?
Is memory usage visible in standard tools (htop, smem)?
Does the app handle swap storms without becoming unresponsive?
Does the app work on ext4, btrfs, XFS, and ZFS?
Are file operations atomic where needed (fsync, rename)?
Does the app handle filesystem quotas?
Are symbolic links followed correctly?
Does the app handle case-sensitive filenames?
Are file permissions set correctly (umask, mode)?
Does the app handle filesystem-specific features (CoW on btrfs)?
Does the app support multiple user sessions on the same machine?
Is user data isolated between Linux users?
Can the daemon run per-user or system-wide?
Does the app handle fast user switching?
Are resources (memory, CPU) fair-shared between users?
Can the app run on multi-seat configurations?
Does the app handle polkit authorization correctly?
Can the app be profiled with perf, strace, and ltrace?
Are performance metrics visible in /proc/[pid]/status?
Does the app support flamegraph generation?
Can the app be traced with eBPF tools?
Are cgroup resource limits respected?
Does the app handle CPU frequency scaling?
Is the app compatible with perf-tools and bcc-tools?

floors.persona_name: Snap vs Flatpak vs AppImage Evaluator
questions: Which packaging format provides the best user experience?
Which format has the smallest download size?
Which format integrates best with desktop environments?
Which format provides the best security sandbox?
Which format supports auto-updates most reliably?
Can all three formats be maintained from a single build pipeline?
Which format is preferred by the Linux community?
Can Solace Browser run on embedded Linux (Yocto, Buildroot)?
Does the app work on minimal Linux installations?
Can the app run on ARM-based SBCs (Pine64, Rock Pi)?
Is the memory footprint small enough for embedded devices?
Can the headless mode run on devices without displays?
Does the app work on read-only root filesystems?
Can the app be embedded in a custom Linux appliance?
Does the app coexist with Steam and Proton without conflicts?
Can the app handle GPU driver switches (Mesa vs proprietary)?
Does the app work on SteamOS / Steam Deck?
Is the app compatible with MangoHud and Gamescope?
Does the app handle Vulkan vs OpenGL selection?
Can the app run alongside full-screen games?
Does the app handle display resolution changes from games?
Can the browser run headless on a Linux server?
Is there a Docker Compose for production deployment?
Can the app handle server-grade workloads (100+ concurrent sessions)?
Does the app work on major cloud providers (AWS, GCP, Azure Linux VMs)?
Can the daemon mode run as a systemd service on servers?
Are log files rotated with logrotate?
Can the app be monitored with Prometheus/Grafana?
Does the app support AT-SPI2 for assistive technology?
Can screen magnifiers (GNOME Zoom, KMag) work with the app?
Does the app support high contrast themes on Linux?
Can the app be operated entirely by keyboard?
Does the app support switch access devices?
Are color filters and inversion compatible?
Does the app support on-screen keyboards (Onboard, Maliit)?
Can developers build from source with standard Linux toolchains?
Does the development setup work with popular editors (VS Code, Neovim)?
Are development dependencies installable via system package manager?
Can developers run the test suite on Linux?
Is the CI/CD pipeline tested on Linux?
Are debugging tools (gdb, valgrind) compatible?
Are developer docs current for Linux-specific setup?
Does Solace Browser feel at home on Linux — not a Windows/Mac port?
Would a Linux power user choose Solace Browser as their daily driver?
Does the Linux build respect the Linux philosophy (do one thing well, composability)?
Is the CLI experience on Linux the best across all platforms?
Does the app contribute to the Linux desktop ecosystem?
Would Phoronix or OMG!Ubuntu feature Solace Browser positively?
Is this the Linux app the Dragon Rider uses on his daily driver?
Can a non-technical Linux user install the app from their software center?
Does the app look and feel familiar in the user's desktop environment?
Can the user find settings and change preferences easily?
Does the app crash or behave oddly in normal use on Linux?
Would the user know how to get help if something goes wrong?
Does the app integrate with the desktop environment naturally?
Would a non-technical Linux user recommend this app to a friend?
